framework.reasoning: Paper explicitly names Qiskit (IBM) for all quantum operations, and sklearn for classical SVM. PyTorch is NOT used for the model itself but is selected as the project scaffolding framework for reproducibility conventions (seeding, data loading). The actual compute stack is: qiskit-machine-learning (ZZFeatureMap, QuantumKernel), qiskit-aer (statevector_simulator), scikit-learn (SVC with precomputed kernel, SelectKBest, KMeans, StandardScaler, train_test_split, metrics). No GPU is required — all computation is CPU-bound quantum simulation. CUDA is therefore not required. HuggingFace is irrelevant. Plain YAML is sufficient for config management given the modest hyperparameter count.

Loads and validates the Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Detection CSV. Returns train/test splits as numpy arrays.

module_hierarchy.description: Preprocessing transforms: normalization (StandardScaler / MinMaxScaler), and any post-SMOTE cleanup.

Quantum-SMOTE implementation. Clusters minority samples with K-means, encodes them via amplitude encoding, computes angular distance via swap test, and synthesizes new samples via quantum rotation. This is the most speculative module (SIR confidence 0.70-0.80); implemented as a swappable abstract base with a concrete QiskitQuantumSMOTE subclass.

public_methods.name: fit_resample
public_methods.signature: fit_resample(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: __init__(n_clusters: int = 5, rotation_angle: float = 0.5, minority_ratio: float = 0.5, segmentation_factor: float = 1.0, random_state: int = 42, backend: str = 'statevector_simulator')

public_methods.name: fit_resample
public_methods.signature: fit_resample(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

public_methods.name: _amplitude_encode
public_methods.returns: Qiskit QuantumCircuit encoding x via amplitude encoding

public_methods.name: fit_resample
public_methods.signature: fit_resample(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

module_hierarchy.description: Wraps Qiskit's ZZFeatureMap. Provides a clean interface for constructing the parameterized quantum circuit used in QSVM. Supports variable qubit counts (4, 8, 10) per paper ablation study.

Wraps Qiskit's QuantumKernel to compute the full N×N kernel matrix K_ij = |<phi(x_i)|phi(x_j)>|^2. Implements swap-test circuit (Eq. 4-7 from paper) for kernel value computation. Caches computed kernel matrices to avoid redundant circuit evaluations.

public_methods.name: compute_kernel_matrix
public_methods.signature: compute_kernel_matrix(X_train: np.ndarray, X_test: np.ndarray | None = None) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.name: kernel_entry
public_methods.returns: Scalar kernel value K(xi, xj) in [0, 1]

public_methods.name: save_kernel
public_methods.returns: None — saves kernel matrix to .npy file for caching

module_hierarchy.description: Top-level QSVM model. Orchestrates feature map → kernel computation → classical SVM solver → decision function. Wraps sklearn SVC(kernel='precomputed'). This is the primary model class.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: __init__(n_qubits: int = 10, reps: int = 2, entanglement: str = 'full', C: float = 1.0, backend: str = 'statevector_simulator', cache_kernel: bool = True, random_state: int = 42)

public_methods.name: predict_proba
public_methods.returns: proba: [N_test, 2] float64 — class probabilities (requires probability=True on SVC)

public_methods.name: decision_function
public_methods.returns: scores: [N_test] float64 — raw SVM decision values

module_hierarchy.description: Models subpackage init. Exports QSVM, QuantumSMOTE, QSVMFeatureMap, QSVMKernelComputer.
Orchestrates the full training pipeline: data loading → preprocessing → Quantum-SMOTE → QSVM fit → checkpoint save. Also supports classical SVM baseline mode for ablation.

module_hierarchy.description: Not applicable for SVM (no gradient loss). Contains SVM dual objective formulation as documentation and utility for computing SVM margin. Kept for pipeline consistency.

tensor_flows.name: Full Training Pipeline (Quantum-SMOTE + QSVM)
steps: 2. FEATURE SELECT via SelectKBest(k=10, score_func=f_classif) → X_reduced: [284807, 10]
4. SCALE via StandardScaler fit on X_train → X_train_scaled: [~227845, 10] in approx [-3, 3]
Apply same scaler to X_test: X_test_scaled: [~56962, 10]
6b. FOR EACH minority sample x_i: amplitude_encode(x_i) → |phi_i>: log2(10) qubits
6e. Repeat until target minority_ratio achieved → X_synthetic: [N_synth, 10]
FOR each pair (x_i, x_j) in X_balanced:

tensor_flows.name: Inference / Prediction Pass
steps: FOR each support vector x_sv in support_vectors:
compute K(x_test, x_sv) = |<phi(x_test)|phi(x_sv)>|^2 via ZZFeatureMap + inner product
3. DECISION: f(x) = sign( sum_i alpha_i * y_i * K(x_test_i, x_sv) + b )

tensor_flows.name: Quantum-SMOTE Synthesis (detail)
steps: 4. SWAP TEST: build ancilla + controlled-SWAP circuit
In classical representation: x_syn = x + rotation_angle * (centroid - x)
(Quantum rotation gate Ry parameterized by angular_distance * segmentation_factor)

tensor_flows.name: Kernel Matrix Computation (ZZFeatureMap path)
steps: 1. Build ZZFeatureMap circuit U_phi parameterized by x_i → circuit_i
2. Build ZZFeatureMap circuit U_phi parameterized by x_j → circuit_j

tensor_flows.name: Ablation: Classical SVM Baseline
steps: 1. FIT SVC(kernel='rbf', C=1.0) on X_train_scaled, y_train
3. Run for D in {4, 8, 10} features to reproduce Table II

_comments.C: ASSUMED: sklearn SVC default C=1.0 — confidence 0.65

_comments.n_clusters: ASSUMED: common default K=5 for KMeans — confidence 0.45
_comments.rotation_angle: ASSUMED: mid-range; paper mentions hyperparameter but not value — confidence 0.45

_comments.n_features: Explicitly stated in paper: top-10 KBest features — confidence 0.90
_comments.score_func: ASSUMED: f_classif (standard for binary classification) — confidence 0.60

entrypoints.purpose: Downloads the Kaggle credit card fraud dataset using kaggle API; requires KAGGLE_USERNAME and KAGGLE_KEY env vars
Runs the full Table I and Table II ablation study: QSVM vs SVM across 4/8/10 qubits, SMOTE vs undersampling

docker_spec._note: No GPU/CUDA required. All quantum simulation is CPU-based via Qiskit Aer statevector simulator.

risk_assessment.description: Quantum-SMOTE circuit implementation is underspecified. The paper describes the concept (swap test + quantum rotation) but does not give the exact gate sequence, qubit count, or rotation gate type for the synthesis step. The Mohanty et al. 2025 reference may have the canonical implementation but was not available for parsing.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement QuantumSMOTE as abstract base class with a configurable, well-documented concrete implementation. Use Ry rotation as primary assumption. Add ClassicalSMOTE fallback. Include a TODO comment flagging the swap-test circuit for verification against Mohanty et al. 2025. Add an integration test comparing output distributions.

risk_assessment.description: Kernel matrix computation is extremely expensive for large N_balanced. The Kaggle dataset has 284K samples. Even with 10 qubits on a statevector simulator, computing an N×N kernel matrix is O(N^2) quantum circuit evaluations — each taking ~0.5-2s on CPU. Training on full balanced set could take hours to days.
risk_assessment.source: Dataset size [284807, 30]; quantum simulation overhead; no compute budget reported in paper
risk_assessment.mitigation: 1) Implement kernel matrix caching (.npy files). 2) Add a --max-train-samples flag to subsample for testing. 3) The paper likely used a small balanced training subset (note confusion matrix suggests ~135K test samples, implying training on subsampled data). 4) Expose a debug config with n_samples=500 for rapid iteration.

risk_assessment.description: SVM regularization parameter C is not specified. C critically controls the fraud/non-fraud tradeoff (precision vs recall). Wrong C could produce results diverging significantly from paper's reported metrics.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to C=1.0 (sklearn default). Add --C flag to train.py CLI. Document clearly in config.yaml that C is assumed. Consider adding a simple grid search script over C in [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100] as scripts/tune_C.py.

risk_assessment.description: Train/test split ratio is unspecified. The reported metrics (accuracy=98.8%) are sensitive to which samples appear in test vs train. A different split could shift results.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to 80/20 stratified split with random_state=42 (reproducible). Expose test_size in config. Note explicitly in README that split is an assumption.

risk_assessment.description: Exact top-10 features from KBest are not enumerated in the paper. The scoring function (chi2, f_classif, mutual_info) affects which features are selected, which changes what the quantum kernel operates on.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement feature selection with configurable score_func. Log and save selected feature names to results/. Run evaluate.py with different score functions to identify which gives best match to paper results.

risk_assessment.description: ZZFeatureMap reps and entanglement pattern are unspecified. These directly affect circuit expressivity and kernel values. Different reps values produce meaningfully different kernel matrices.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to Qiskit defaults (reps=2, full entanglement). Expose both as config parameters. Add ablation script over reps in {1, 2, 3}.

risk_assessment.description: Dataset requires Kaggle account to download. The creditcard.csv is publicly available on Kaggle but requires API key authentication. This creates a setup friction point.
risk_assessment.source: Evaluation protocol: Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide download.sh using kaggle CLI. Document setup steps clearly in README. Include a data/README_data.md with manual download instructions as fallback.

risk_assessment.description: Qiskit versioning: qiskit-machine-learning and qiskit-aer have had significant API changes across versions. The QuantumKernel API in particular was restructured between qiskit-machine-learning 0.6 and 0.7.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Pin exact versions in requirements.txt. Test against pinned versions only. Add version compatibility note to README.
